codeReview.summary: The fix successfully adds deterministic nonce prepopulation hooks and the A-side pending-operations invariance assertions, but the denial round-trip test still does not strictly enforce the contract's 15-second failure bound from the moment B dispatches sign.

issues.description: The contract requires B to receive OperationFailure within 15s of dispatch (validation-contract.md:581-584), but in the `requestId: null` path the spec first waits up to 30s to discover `signRequestId` (lines 680-761) and only then starts the 15s timeout (`failureWaitStartedAt` at line 864). This can pass even when failure arrives well after 15s from dispatch, so the timing bound from the original failure is still not fully enforced.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture docs still describe a local A-side `peer_denied` runtime event as current behavior, which conflicts with the narrowed contract and current runtime reality used by these tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md (Data Flow: Approval Prompts) says denied requests emit a local `peer_denied` runtime event on A, while validation-contract.md:581-586 and the updated policy-denial-roundtrip spec comments explicitly state this A-side runtime event is not currently available.

summary: Fix review failed: nonce prepopulation and A-side pending invariance were added, but the strict 15s dispatch-to-failure bound remains partially unenforced in the null-requestId branch.
